name: Jalapeno Breadsticks
recipeIngredient: 6 Jalapeno chiles (fresh or canned), seeded and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Saute onion in butter over medium heat until lightly browned. Remove from heat and set aside.
2. Lightly grease three large baking sheets and sprinkle with cornmeal. In a mixing bowl, sprinkle yeast over warm water. Let stand until dissolved, then stir in sugar, salt, chopped jalapenos, sauteed onion, buttermilk, and 2 cups of the flour. Beat mixture well.
3. Stir in enough of the remaining flour to make dough easy to handle. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface; knead about 5 minutes. Place in a large greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1-1/2 to 2 hours.
4. Punch down dough and divide into 4 equal parts. On a floured surface, roll out one part dough to form an 8-inch x 8-inch square. Using a knife or pizza cutter, cut the square into 8 equal strips. Place the strips onto prepared baking sheet. Repeat with remaining portions of dough.
5. Let rise uncovered for 1 hour (for light, airy breadsticks) or 30 minutes (for denser, chewier texture). Bake in a preheated 400°F oven for 10 to 12 minutes, or until golden brown.
